rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK C FIECHTER VS. CARL WESCOTT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 03, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT CARL WESCOTT's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Off calendar. Defendants did not serve and file the motion at least 16 court days before the hearing plus 5 days for service by mail, as required by Code Civ. Proc. § 1005(b). =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MPL BRANDS, INC. VS. BRONCO WINE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration *** Redacted Version Invalidated Pursuant To Order Denying Respondent'S Motion For Order Sealing Records Filed 12/3/19***
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 03, 2019, Line 2, RESPONDENT BRONCO WINE COMPANY Petition To Compel Arbitration *** Redacted Version ***. Bronco Wine Co.'s petition to compel arbitration is granted as to Magave Tequila, Inc. and denied as to MPL Brands NV, Inc. An agency relationship between a party to an arbitration agreement and a nonsignatory may impose a duty to arbitrate on the latter. (Murphy v. Direct TV (9th Cir. 2013) 724 F.3d 1218, 1232.) Here, to carry out its sales agreement with Bronco, signatory MPL Brands, Inc. (MPL) required licenses and permits held by nonsignatory Magave. Indeed, MPL represents in the agreement that MPL "possesses" those licenses and permits. (Agmt. 2.) In contrast, Bronco's "alter ego" allegations prove no wrongdoing or disregard of the corporate form regarding Magave or MPL Brands NV. Nor is Magave a third-party beneficiary, so it could not be compelled to arbitrate under that theory. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Bronco Wine Co. is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MPL BRANDS, INC. VS. BRONCO WINE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Sealing Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 03, 2019, Line 2, RESPONDENT BRONCO WINE COMPANY's Motion For Order Sealing Records. Bronco Wine Co.'s motion for order sealing records is denied. Court records are presumed to be open and Bronco fails to make the showings required for sealing. (CRC 2.550(d).) Further, the agreement Bronco seeks to seal has been in the public record for a year, so any confidentiality has been waived. (See Savaglio v. Wal-Mart Stores, Inc. (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 588, 598-601.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for MPL Brands, Inc. is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: AURORA BIOPHARMA, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Second Petition And Second Petition To Compel Discovery And Sanctions Against Non-Party Celgene, Corp.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER AURORA BIOPHARMA, INC.'s Second Petition To Compel Discovery And Sanctions Against Non-Party Celgene, Corp. Pro Tem Judge Peter Vestal, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The tentative ruling exceeds the system character limits. The Pro Tem Judge will email the parties and/or their counsel the tentative ruling prior to 3 pm. If any party does not receive the tentative ruling by 3 pm, please email the Pro Tem Judge at pvestal@nvlawllp.com and cc: all other parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pvestal@nvlawllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion{s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion, and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JUUL LABS, INC VS. SIDDHARTH BREJA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner Juul Labs, Inc'S Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 03, 2019, Line 3, PETITIONER JUUL LABS, INC's Petition To Compel Arbitration. Juul Labs, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is granted. Respondent Siddharth Breja and Juul contracted to arbitrate "any claim, controversy or legal dispute." (Johnsrud Dec. Ex. B.) To avoid arbitration, Breja had to show both procedural and substantive unconscionability. (See Baltazar v. Forever 21 (2016) 62 Cal.4th 1237, 1243.) As to the former, Breja declares that he had no chance to negotiate employment terms. However, Breja pled in federal court that he negotiated terms with Juul and it "agreed to" his "proposed changes." (Dhillon Dec. Ex. A 10:26-28.) As to the latter, the parties applied CCP §1280 et seq. to their arbitration and thus agreed to a neutral arbitrator, discovery and a written award. (Id. at §§1281.6, 1283.4, 1283.05.) CCP §1281.2(c) does not apply as Breja is not litigating with a third party. Arbitration is compelled because the Federal Arbitration Act governs the parties' agreement. (Rodriguez v. American Technologies, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 1110, 1115, 1117-22.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: LABOR COMMISSIONER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PHILLIP ACHILLES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement; Declaration Of Matthew Sirolly In Support Thereof; Exhibits A To D.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 03, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFF LABOR COMMISSIONER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement. Plaintiff Labor Commissioner of the State of California's motion to enforce settlement agreement pursuant to CCP §664.6 is granted; no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The court will sign the proposed order.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: HAIQING QIU VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 03, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the first amended complaint (FAC) is granted without leave to amend. (1) Plaintiff does not dispute that he failed to comply with the Government Tort Claims Act, (2) the FAC fails to allege a breach of legal duty and (3) "mayors are not vicariously liable for injuries caused by the act or omission of the public entity or advisory body" (Govt. Code §820.9.). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN A. BARTHOLOMEW VS. GOLDEN GATE TOW, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 03, 2019, Line 12, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE TOW, INC., THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY, DANIEL STONE's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer and motion to strike are ordered off calendar. CCP §§430.41 and 435.5 require that counsel "shall meet and confer in person or by telephone" before filing a demurrer or motion to strike. Counsel are ordered to comply with the law. The parties shall immediately meet and confer and defendants' response to the 2nd Amended Complaint is due by January 3, 2020. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH P STOLLER MD VS. DENNIS HERRERA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 03, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANT KIMBERLY KIRCHMEYER, CHRISTOPHER SCHULTZ's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Kimberly Kirchmeyer and Christopher Shultz's demurrer to the second amended complaint's first, second and third causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs effectively concede that they have failed to exhaust administrative remedies. (See Contractors' State License Board v. Sup. Ct. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 771, 778-79.) Plaintiffs nonetheless urge me to bypass the administrative proceedings using a "public policy exception" that appellate courts sometimes invoke, usually without calling it that. However, I believe a factual record developed in the administrative proceedings would be beneficial to reasoned adjudication of this dispute. Adding "JK" as a plaintiff does not avoid the exhaustion bar; she may seek to intervene in the administrative proceedings. (Govt. Code §11440.50.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)
